item.title: Drag queen Pattie Gonia says Patagonia has launched trademark suit. Here's what we know
item.description: A drag queen and a major outdoor clothing brand have a fight over trademarks. Here's the feud explained.

item.title: Friends, loyal listeners gather for James Valentine's public memorial
item.description: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after he was diagnosed with cancer.

item.title: FIFA World Cup selection for Michael Woud forces wedding postponement
item.description: Auckland FC goalkeeper Michael Woud has had to put his wedding plans on hold after being selected in New Zealand's squad for the World Cup, but he says taking part in soccer's biggest tournament is not a bad reason to cancel.

item.title: Plummeting gold price impacts Australian dollar as Iran war pushes it into bear market
item.description: The gold price is now plunging after the precious metal's surge to a record high this year.

item.title: AFL backs controversial decision to disallow Hawks' goal against Saints
item.description: A controversial umpiring decision that disallowed Hawthorn star Nick Watson's goal against St Kilda has been backed by the AFL.

item.title: Mouse plague taskforce to help WA country schools battling infestation
item.description: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

item.title: Angela Jones-Blayney defends appointment of 'Pink Lady' mentee to Parramatta council
item.description: A Western Sydney local government executive undermined recruitment processes to hire a woman she mentored for years and socialised with as part of a networking group dubbed the Pink Ladies.

item.title: Bumper year at Northern Territory's biggest bull sale delivers average price of nearly $5,000 a head
item.description: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

item.title: Former prime minister Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president
item.description: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.

item.title: New Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo unlikely to be grand slam boss
item.description: Incoming Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo appears unlikely to take on the double workload of his predecessor Craig Tiley, by running both the national organisation and the Australian Open tournament.

item.title: NT Aboriginal families in court bid to be recognised as traditional owners of Cobourg Peninsula
item.description: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

item.title: Scott calls for high ARC standards, laments missed 'howler' in loss
item.description: Geelong's coach takes aim at the AFL's controversial review technology after yet another blunder cost his side dearly in the loss to Carlton.

item.title: Friday finance with Lin Lin
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Kids still see gambling ads during late night sport under Labor
item.description: The partial ad ban will not prevent children from seeing gambling ads during footy or rugby games, Labor's draft legislation shows.

item.title: Blues rookie in doubt for Origin II after injury in Cronulla's win over Manly
item.description: New South Wales are set to be without exciting hooker Blayke Brailey after he suffered a suspected broken arm in Cronulla's 28-22 win over Manly.

item.title: WHO says 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths from Bundibugyo strain of Ebola
item.description: The outbreak is threatening to overwhelm health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo, while nine cases have now been confirmed in neighbouring Uganda.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of John Farnham, 60s pop and where to live on the Moon
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Best new books out in May from Elizabeth Strout, Francesca Albanese and more
item.description: Sweet teen love, gay football stars, alien abductions and a murderous PhD student bent on revenge — this month's best new releases have it all.

item.title: Australians limited to keyhole view of Bondi attack scrutiny
item.description: For now, Australians seeking to understand any intelligence gaps, failures, missed warnings and government failings before the Bondi massacre are expected to accept a keyhole view.

item.title: Warm winter outlook not bothering ski resorts as 2026 snow season looms
item.description: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.

item.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say were failed by AFL
item.description: Several families tell the ABC they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.

item.title: New artificial homes help Kangaroo Island dunnarts recover after bushfire
item.description: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.

item.title: Do AI-generated images have any place in Australian politics?
item.description: A Victorian state MP's use of an apparently AI-generated image raises questions about whether AI has any place in politics.

item.title: Mount Isa mum calls for more support after birth of premature twins
item.description: Mount Isa mother Kristen Curd spent four months in Townsville after giving birth to premature twin boys, and says the hardest part began when they finally came home.

item.title: First of five survivors rescued from flooded Laos cave
item.description: Rescue divers in Laos have safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

item.title: Businessman David Penman lays further charges against Hepburn councillor in private prosecution
item.description: A private prosecution launched by a businessman in Victoria's Hepburn Shire has been described as a "nightmare" by a lawyer defending one of two local government figures facing charges.

item.title: Booking.com is being sued in Europe, but Aussie travellers aren't included
item.description: Booking.com is being sued in Europe, and consumer advocates think Aussie travellers might also have a claim.

item.title: 'Notorious' Gold Coast frontman for offshore shell companies faces extradition to US
item.description: A Queensland jetski salesman who helped set up offshore shell companies embroiled in scandals involving Mexican drug cartels and North Korean arms dealers faces extradition to the United States on money laundering charges.

item.title: The Interview: David Szalay on why AI won't be able to replace authors
item.description: The Booker Prize winner sat down at the Sydney Writers' Festival to ponder the threat of AI to fiction, share some recommendations and consider whether his book was really about masculinity.

item.title: Scientists target rabbit fertility in radical new pest-control plan
item.description: Scientists are using rabbit IVF and gene-editing technology to crash Australia's feral rabbit population by spreading infertility through female rabbits.

item.title: French Open: Alex de Minaur and Novak Djokovic eliminated as seeded stars continue struggles in heat
item.description: Seeded stars continue to tumble at Roland-Garros as both Alex de Minaur and Novak Djokovic's campaigns end in the third round.

item.title: Whyalla community cautiously optimistic about steelworks sequel
item.description: "We've seen this movie before" is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.

item.title: Diphtheria cases surged a month before NT Health issued public alert, leaked data shows
item.description: Internal Northern Territory Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.

item.title: Colourism in the Pacific continues as region grapples with history of colonialism
item.description: Skin bleaching and lightening products are widely sold in Tonga, which is part of a region with a long history of colourism.

item.title: Tasmania chosen to supply blood samples for global Parkinson's disease study
item.description: Experts are predicting Parkinson's disease to double in the next 20 years, but little is known about what causes the condition and how to cure it. Tasmania has been chosen to contribute blood samples to a global study aimed at improving understanding of the genetics behind Parkinson's disease.

item.title: Quarantine flight, mouse plague and space dreams feature in WA May photo picks
item.description: From Perth's latest quarantine experience to a cultural burial on Rottnest Island, here are our top ABC News photos for Western Australia in May.

item.title: Gold Coast beach erosion sparks metal-detecting boom with some making 'about $1,000 a month'
item.description: Some Gold Coast metal detectorists say they are making $1,000 a month from treasures found on beaches eroded by storms.

item.title: Artist and surfer Jessi Rebel's passions collide at Australian Indigenous Surfing Titles
item.description: Surfer Jessi Rebel's journeys with art and identity have beaten similar paths — both inspired by one relative's sage piece of advice.

item.title: Latest politicians' pitch for Collie may not hold up under scrutiny
item.description: The WA government's proposed defence hub is the latest in a long line of plans for Collie that have delivered mixed results.

item.title: Adolfo Daniel Vallejo facing monster fine after saying French Open match 'needs to be umpired by a man'
item.description: Adolfo Daniel Vallejo faces a massive fine after making sexist remarks aimed at a chair umpire at Roland-Garros.

item.title: Guardian Girls Karate takes up the fight against gender-based violence
item.description: Savannah Last has been a "karate kid" since she was two, but when she was attacked by a group of teenagers last year, she felt "hopeless".

item.title: Two Tasmanian primary health services receive funding reprieve after budget cuts
item.description: Two southern Tasmanian primary health services have been thrown a one-year lifeline, but the state government says it wants the federal government to step in and fund the programs beyond that.

item.title: Transform tinned tuna into a crunchy, golden dinner - ABC News
item.description: Kishwar Chowdhury says this nostalgic dish is "the ultimate Bangladeshi diaspora invention".

item.title: This road was Russia's key logistics route but now it's a 'highway to hell'
item.description: Ukraine has developed AI-enabled drones capable of making precision strikes from hundreds of kilometres away, and it means using Russia's vital logistics routes has become a dangerous "lottery".

item.title: Cattle prices kick as rain tumbles in the eastern states
item.description: Cattle prices jumped significantly this week as rain fell across key production areas, especially in New South Wales.

item.title: New generation embraces permaculture, which took root in 1970s oil crisis
item.description: The tenets of permaculture are attracting a new generation of home gardeners. It's perhaps no surprise, considering the movement has firm roots in the world's last oil crisis in the 1970s.

item.title: Inside the inner circle that fuelled Matthew Perry's ketamine addiction
item.description: A trusted assistant, two doctors, a middleman and the "Ketamine Queen": How Matthew Perry's inner circle preyed on the Friends star's addiction before his death.

item.title: Iga Świątek urges French Open officials to move advertising boards after Zeynep Sönmez's nasty fall
item.description: Iga Świątek urges French Open officials to move advertising boards away from the courts after seeing Zeynep Sönmez's tournament end due to injury.

item.title: Dogs hang on for victory over Pies after frenetic finish
item.description: The Bulldogs dig deep to to notch a hard-fought four-point victory over a surging Collingwood at Docklands, after the Dockers go top of the ladder with a win over troubled premiers Brisbane.

item.title: Will AI be conscious in the future? Here's what a philosopher and a neuroscientist think
item.description: The question "can computers think" is something many people are taking very seriously. A philosophy professor believes we could be heading towards a future with conscious machines, while a neuroscientist is sceptical.

item.title: Pete Hegseth tells IISS Shangri-La Dialogue that US won't allow China to dominate Asia
item.description: The US Defense Secretary addressed Asia's most important defence summit, but did not mention Taiwan as questions remain over a stalled weapons package.

item.title: Tigers 'dodged a bullet' as Bulldogs fume over controversial ref calls
item.description: Canterbury Bulldogs coach Cameron Ciraldo has been left fuming at several refereeing decisions that went against his side in their 22-16 loss to Wests Tigers.

item.title: AFL concedes umpire error after 'howler' in Geelong-Carlton clash
item.description: Geelong's Ollie Dempsey was denied a clear goal in the narrow loss to Carlton when umpires chose not to call on the AFL Review Centre to check a decision.

item.title: Lil' Kim brings nostalgia to her Vivid Sydney show at Carriageworks as festival continues
item.description: Rap superstar Lil' Kim performed mostly truncated versions of her hits at her Vivid Sydney show, but she gave the diehard fans in the audience exactly what they came for.

item.title: Donald Trump's doctor says he is in 'excellent health'. Here's what's in the president's medical report
item.description: Donald Trump's medical report shows the US president underwent a CT scan, cancer screenings and other preventative assessments carried out by 22 specialists.

item.title: Angus Taylor draws 'battle lines' against Labor in key Liberal Party address
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor accuses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese of starting a war on aspiration with the recent federal budget.

item.title: Human remains found in search for missing Scottsdale man Peter Willoughby, police say
item.description: Tasmania Police has confirmed a member of a volunteer search group has found what are believed to be human remains in the search for a missing man in Tasmania's north.

item.title: Liverpool sack manager Arne Slot, who won Premier League in his first season, after fifth-place finish
item.description: The Dutchman won the Premier League title in his first season, but failed to live up to expectations the following year as Liverpool finished fifth.

item.title: Former teachers' pets, rejoice. Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz is back
item.description: Welcome back to your Sunday morning quiz with everyone's favourite word wonderboy.

item.title: Five days in darkness left scientist Kiana Aran thinking about anything but science
item.description: Spending big money to do nothing in the dark is on the rise. Scientist Kiana Aran chronicled what happened to her when she tried it.

item.title: Four theatre shows take on Jane Austen's classic novel Pride and Prejudice
item.description: In a modern-day cost-of-living crisis, the classic 1817 novel's tale of inheritance and wealth still rings true.

item.title: What negative gearing and transgender rights reveal about the Liberals' populist strategy
item.description: The Coalition's answer to the rise of conservative populism has been to treat it primarily as a cultural phenomenon. Under Angus Taylor, that instinct appears to be hardening.

item.title: Is the government really overhauling employment services, or tinkering at the edges of a broken system?
item.description: The government isn't rebuilding the Commonwealth Employment Service, but will its proposed changes fix a broken system?

item.title: Senior footballer sexually assaulted child on end-of-season trip. He wasn't the one who left the club
item.description: When a man who sexually assaulted a minor on a post-season football trip was welcomed back to the club with open arms, Megan decided she was done.

item.title: Schools in Asia are embracing artificial intelligence in classrooms
item.description: Countries in Asia are emerging as the world's early adopters of AI in education, as the global race to lead in the technology accelerates.

item.title: Mothers fear children's chronic illnesses are linked to bushfire smoke during pregnancy
item.description: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.

item.title: Periods prove a big barrier for disabled women's participation in sport
item.description: Vanessa was forced to regularly skip sport because of her period. New research shows that more than 60 per cent of disabled athletes have done the same.

item.title: French Open men's draw back to glorious chaos with no clear favourite
item.description: All the key favourites are now out of the men's draw at the French Open, but then again, this tournament has always been just a little bit different.

item.title: Nearly 80 years after Bitter Springs film, its Aboriginal actors are finally being understood
item.description: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

item.title: History-making mayor of Maribyrnong, Mohamed Semra, is only getting started
item.description: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.

item.title: Life and death in the outback: Why Australians are still dying in the bush
item.description: Kelly’s son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She’s on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal on penalties in Champions League final
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal in a tense shootout in the Champions League final to be crowned European champions for the second straight year.

item.title: Whitsundays artist creates fish leather from seafood waste
item.description: In the front yard of her home in the picturesque Whitsundays, Felicity Chapman strips the last pieces of flesh and sinew from a strip of barramundi skin to make wearable leather art.

item.title: Toxic sludge in Homebush Bay, 1995
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: University students bioremediate Agent Orange chemicals manufactured for Vietnam War from Sydney waterways
item.description: More than 50 years after the Vietnam War, students are working to remove Agent Orange remnants from a waterway connected to Sydney Harbour, where the chemicals were manufactured.

item.title: Pollution along Parramatta River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina beaten by Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff's French Open defence ended by Anastasia Potapova
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka ends Australia's campaign at the French Open with a straightforward win over Daria Kasatkina, while Coco Gauff joins the slew of big names knocked out early in Paris.

item.title: Anniversary of national parks' return to traditional owners celebrated on NSW South Coast
item.description: The Yuin people on the NSW South Coast celebrate two decades of Aboriginal ownership of two national parks.

item.title: Lights at Canberra's Manuka Oval lean inwards thanks to strict planning rule
item.description: If the six light poles skirting Manuka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.

item.title: Mr Skelly animations tackling human themes of death, loss winning over fans worldwide
item.description: An animation series tackling themes of grief, regret, love and gratitude has attracted an online fanbase of millions — and reflects the artist's search for peace and beauty.

item.title: NT Writers Festival launches story of Timorese asylum seekers who took fishing boat to Australia
item.description: Jose da Costa's escape from Indonesian-occupied East Timor in 1995 has been detailed in a new book launched at the Northern Territory Writers Festival this weekend.

item.title: Family gifts historical collection of images captured by pioneering WA photographer Bert Saw
item.description: Pioneering photographer Bert Saw captured Federation-era life and experiences in Denmark, Albany and surrounding communities throughout the 1900s.

item.title: Madeleine Ogilvie's downfall started with a seemingly innocuous question, and the end came quickly
item.description: It started with a seemingly innocuous question. Over the course of several months, Madeleine Ogilvie had many opportunities to provide clarification, but didn't until last week. Then the end came quickly.

item.title: Australia loses first Pakistan ODI, undone by debutant spinner Arafat Minhas
item.description: Australia drops the first game in its limited-overs series in Pakistan as spinner Arafat Minhas takes 5-32 in his maiden one-day international.

item.title: Budget won't be 'main driver' if house prices fall, housing minister says
item.description: The housing minister says the federal budget will have only a "mild" impact on house prices, and if predictions bear out that house prices may drop by 10 per cent, that won't be due to the government's tax changes.

item.title: Police find autistic boy who went missing in wild weather in Perth overnight
item.description: An 11-year-old autistic boy, who is non-verbal, has been found after going missing from Perth's western suburbs in wild weather on Saturday afternoon.

item.title: Socceroos defeated by Mexico despite encouraging second-half showing
item.description: The Socceroos are beaten 1-0 by Mexico in their penultimate World Cup warm up match despite an improved and impressive second-half performance at the Rose Bowl.

item.title: Australian Dance Theatre premieres RISE at Bundanon Art Museum
item.description: The Australian Dance Theatre's first Indigenous artistic director had some dark days after the Voice referendum, but he shines a light on reconciliation in a new outdoor performance set to the rhythm of the rising Sun.

item.title: Hundreds arrested in Paris after violent clashes as Paris Saint-Germain wins Champions League
item.description: More than 50 police officers are wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalise shops, with one small group even trying to storm a Paris police station.

item.title: Love story: Locking down the 'one that got away'
item.description: For a long time, I considered my teenage crush the one that got away. That all changed when we reconnected two decades later.

item.title: Man taken to hospital after tree falls on car, as WA prepares for more wild weather
item.description: Two people have had a lucky escape after a tree fell onto their car on a major highway in WA's South West, as strong winds lashed the region.

item.title: US fires missile into commercial ship trying to reach Iran, Central Command says
item.description: The US military fires a missile into the engine room of the merchant vessel trying to break through its blockade of Iranian ports, the Central Command says.

item.title: Team 'astonished' as Lorena Wiebes kicked out of Giro d'Italia Women after winning stage one
item.description: Lorena Wiebes powered to victory on stage one of Giro d'Italia Women, but was later kicked off the race for having an underweight bike, a finding that shocked her team.

item.title: Haas laments Broncos' BS as Maguire warns against hoping for another miracle
item.description: After four straight losses, the 5-7 Broncos are in the same position as last year before launching an audacious run to the premiership, but coach Michael Maguire says the team should not expect to be able to do that again.

item.title: Why are e-bikes suddenly all over Australia's streets?
item.description: When Barnaby Joyce was deputy prime minister and transport minister in 2021, his assistant minister Kevin Hogan made a quiet, but hugely significant change to the Road Vehicle Standards Act.

item.title: As Iran's internet comes online after months-long blackout, first posts send anti-regime videos viral
item.description: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.

item.title: Tasmania Police 'open' to learning from volunteer group that has found remains in two missing person cases
item.description: A renewed search by volunteers for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby led to the discovery of remains on Saturday. It's the second time this year a group of volunteers has made a significant find during the search for a missing person.

item.title: Australia to buy three second-hand United States submarines under AUKUS shake-up
item.description: The US, UK and Australia have announced they will "streamline" the purchase, with Australia no longer buying new submarines, and acquiring three second-hand ones.

item.title: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues
item.description: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.

item.title: Photos emerge of rescued Laos divers smiling and in good spirits as search continues for two others
item.description: The remaining four men from a group of five were freed from the flooded cave on Saturday evening, in extraordinary and emotional scenes, as search efforts continue for two people thought to still be trapped inside.

item.title: Federal minister Murray Watt rejects two applications to protect Victoria Park ahead of Olympic stadium construction
item.description: The federal government has rejected two applications for protection of Brisbane's Victoria Park ahead of construction on the Olympic stadium beginning tomorrow, but is still considering another five.

item.title: Horses spooked by fireworks bolt through Rome
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Horses bolt through streets of Rome after police officer lights unauthorised fireworks
item.description: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

item.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize Beaufort castle
item.description: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

item.title: Young Australians reject budget and One Nation popularity climbs further in new poll
item.description: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 1: Quiz: Will you be crowned the history champ?
item.description: Who took the first five-wicket haul in one-day cricket? And which state capital was the last to get ABC TV?

item.title: Ian Thorpe on learning to fit in after a career spent standing out
item.description: Ian Thorpe became a global star as a teenager, attracting both opportunities and scrutiny. Now, he wishes he'd done some things differently.

item.title: Family lawyers say there's growing interest in 'prenups'. Here's how they work
item.description: Binding financial agreements take time and money to obtain. Here's how they work.

item.title: Experts warn Australia a 'perfect' target for new 'ghost proxy' Islamist terror network
item.description: Australia would be the "perfect" target for a fledgling Islamist terror network that has carried out antisemitic attacks in Europe, the UK and Canada, experts have warned.

item.title: Myanmar blast kills dozens in rebel-held village, mining explosives blamed
item.description: Authorities say the blast was likely caused by explosives used for mining that had been stored in a warehouse.

item.title: How Trials grew up, got sober and confronted his past on debut solo album Hendle
item.description: The prolific A.B. Original rapper-producer talks about learning to "marry the wrong feeling with the right music".

item.title: Virgin customers have one month to use COVID travel credits
item.description: The airline had more than $90 million in unspent travel credits on its books earlier this year.

item.title: A police assault put a man in the ICU. Then came the cover-up
item.description: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

item.title: National home values flatline in May for the first time since January 2025
item.description: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

item.title: Liberal Party quietly publishes paper grappling with party's failure to attract women, young people
item.description: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.

item.title: Is this the tax reform hill Albanese and Chalmers want to die on?
item.description: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a large majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?

item.title: Italian and Brazilian authorities investigate possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows
item.description: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

item.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut' of festival takings
item.description: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.

item.title: Women reveal Byron Bay's predatory 2000s culture of rape, humiliation and shame
item.description: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to join dozens of others speaking out about the experiences of young girls in Byron Bay in the early 2000s, when they say it became normalised for older boys and men to groom and have sex with girls, then publicly humiliate them.

item.title: Kmart and Shein claim designs Sabo Skirt said they copied were not original
item.description: Shein and Kmart have fired back at Sabo Skirt in an intellectual property dispute, saying the designs have been accused of copying are substantially similar to pre-existing designs.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: Trump demands changes to peace plan as Israel pushes ahead
item.description: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.

item.title: Socceroos reveal 26-player squad for 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

item.title: Officeworks staff say plan to offshore Sydney jobs all about replacing them with cheap labour
item.description: The company says it wants a "globally connected team", but disgruntled staff say plans to offshore jobs is about replacing them with cheap labour.

item.title: Natural hydrogen potential like 'tripping over gold', SA minister says
item.description: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

item.title: Concerns native forests could be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option as it moves away from coal
item.description: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.

item.title: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement sued over alleged inhumane conditions at detention camp
item.description: Civil rights groups allege people at the largest immigration detention centre in Texas are being beaten, abused and denied medical care at the camp.

item.title: ASX 200 finishes flat after clawing back early losses, oil higher — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 has finished largely flat after clawing back early losses, while oil prices rose as investors continued to monitor tensions in the Middle East. Look back on our business blog.

item.title: Cattle company inadvertently tipped off Queensland DPI to biosecurity breach
item.description: A NSW company tipped off authorities to the illegal movement of its cattle, triggering the investigation that led to the largest-ever biosecurity fine under a Queensland law.

item.title: Swifts Creek Bush Nursing Centre after-hours nursing cuts worry residents
item.description: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.

item.title: Australian Jai Hindley third at Giro d'Italia as Jonas Vingegaard completes Grand Tour set
item.description: Australian Jai Hindley finishes third at the Giro d'Italia, while Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard adds a title in Rome to his victories in France and Spain.

item.title: WA storms leave thousands without power as wild weekend weather continues
item.description: Thousands of homes are without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roof torn off two apartment buildings, after WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

item.title: Defence Minister says Australia could still buy five US submarines under AUKUS
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

item.title: Joao Fonseca reaches first grand slam quarterfinal while Iga Świątek is out of French Open
item.description: Nineteen-year-old Joao Fonseca beats two-time French Open runner-up Casper Ruud to reach his first grand slam quarterfinal, while four-time champion Iga Świątek suffers her earliest exit since 2019.

item.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City wins FA Cup, beating Dario Vidošić's Brighton and Hove Albion
item.description: The Women's FA Cup has a distinctly Australian flavour with Mary Fowler's Manchester City thumping Charlize Rule's Brighton and Hove Albion, coached by compatriot Dario Vidošić, whose father was honoured by fans at Wembley.

item.title: Large flock of critically endangered swift parrots seen near Port Macquarie
item.description: The sighting of critically endangered parrots is seen as "really exciting" after hardly any were seen across NSW last year. But a bird expert warns the species still faces significant threats.

item.title: Antisemitism royal commission rejects federal government's bid to keep cabinet documents confidential
item.description: Commissioner Virginia Bell says the documents will provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror threat level was raised to "probable".

item.title: Greens stalwart Bob Brown joins fight against small South West sand mine
item.description: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

item.title: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff faces estimates scrutiny after resignation of minister Madeleine Ogilvie
item.description: "You can ask it in 50 different ways … I won't be commenting any further", the Tasmanian premier says as he responds to questioning in parliament from Labor and the Greens.

item.title: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, says government is failing on First Nations policy
item.description: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, saying she has lost faith in the territory government's approach to Indigenous affairs.

item.title: Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's French Open dinner for black players part of 'an old tradition'
item.description: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

item.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment
item.description: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

item.title: Police officers in Mount Isa shoot dog in street
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tourism operators say AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north
item.description: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

item.title: Mass flowering of Gebang palms in Darwin described as 'once-in-a-lifetime' natural event
item.description: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

item.title: Memory failure contributed to deaths of children left in hot cars, NSW coroner finds
item.description: The deaths of two children who were left in cars during hot weather were the result of their parents' memory failure, a NSW coroner finds.

item.title: Search for 31-year-old man missing in East Arnhem Land ends after nine days
item.description: After a nine-day operation covering more than 1,300 square kilometres of land, the search for a 31-year-old man in East Arnhem Land has been stood down.

item.title: Thousands of baby green sea turtles head to sea after successful egg relocation
item.description: The project at Raine Island, where 90 per cent of northern green sea turtles nest, has been working for a decade to improve hatching success.

item.title: Nancy Guthrie's disappearance remains mystery four months after she vanished
item.description: It has been four months since Nancy Guthrie, the mother of America's Today show co-host Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing. This is what we know about the alleged abduction.

item.title: Historic paddle-steamer PS Canally returns to working life with love from volunteers
item.description: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

item.title: Severe weather warning for Adelaide and South Australia
item.description: The State Emergency Service has advised people to move vehicles away from trees and secure loose items around properties as South Australia braces for damaging winds.

item.title: Easy and affordable curries to bring some winter warmth
item.description: Curries are perfect for cooler months, and many come with a serving of immune-boosting properties.

item.title: Reconciliation Week puts spotlight on Welcome to Country tradition after divisive Anzac Day services
item.description: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

item.title: $6 million of cocaine found near burnt-out truck at boat ramp in Whitsundays
item.description: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.

item.title: Australia wins women's and men's rugby sevens final at World Championship leg in Spain
item.description: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.

item.title: Police to search 1,000 mine shafts, drain dam in renewed search for Trisha Graf
item.description: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

item.title: Teenagers on NSW Central Coast create student-led Northwave music festival
item.description: High school students have put on a music festival as part of their studies to get them into the industry.

item.title: International Ebola cases climbing 'fast' but Australia won't impose travel restrictions
item.description: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

item.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over death of mental health patient at RDH
item.description: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

item.title: Maroons lose Pat Carrigan and Gehamat Shibasaki ahead of State of Origin II against Blues
item.description: Queensland's task of levelling the State of Origin series has become more difficult after confirmation two of its stars have been ruled out of Game II at the MCG.

item.title: Euphoria season 3 finale recap: Guns, drugs, the bible and Rue's fate
item.description: Here's your recap of season 3, episode 8 of Euphoria. There are spoilers. You have been warned.

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes amid negotiations to end war
item.description: The United States says it struck Iranian military and Iran says it responded by targeting a US base.

item.title: Woman rescued after being stuck in sand
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Woman rescued after getting stuck in quicksand at Glenelg North shares warning
item.description: A relaxing sunset walk at the beach with a friend turned to panic for a young woman after her leg got stuck in quicksand, prompting a rescue from police and fire crews.

item.title: Friends recreate historic journey from Adelaide to Darwin in 1925 Studebaker cars
item.description: If you saw a car in the central Australian outback you wouldn't expect it to be a vintage 1925 Studebaker ... let alone two of them, driven by two men called Brenton.

item.title: AI chatbots accused of helping to encourage violent attacks and at the centre of US lawsuits
item.description: An investigation into how AI chatbots respond to violent requests produces "deeply disturbing" results, as OpenAI denies ChatGPT encourage crime.

item.title: Influencer recorded chat with Bankstown nurses for 'protection', court told
item.description: An Israeli content creator involved in an online video chat where two Sydney nurses allegedly threatened Israeli patients tells a court he recorded his chats for his own protection and published their conversation because he was "really worried about their statements".

item.title: Snowy Monaro Regional Council CEO accuses Andrew Thaler of bullying
item.description: A Snowy Monaro councillor has accused the industrial relations commission of sanitising his statements in a case over bullying of his council's CEO.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: 'Awestruck' Grenvold bolts into the Diamonds squad
item.description: Despite signing her first full-time Super Netball contract this year there was no guarantee Jessie Grenvold would get a lot of court time. But through injury, the Mavericks defender took on a starting spot and is now an Australian Diamond.

item.title: Dog rescued off island after being swept into ocean at Batemans Bay
item.description: The large pooch swam 800 metres to a nearby island after being swept off rocks at Batemans Bay.

item.title: Dog rescued after being swept off rocks
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Godsend': Queensland hospice needs funding boost to keep end-of-life service alive
item.description: Hervey Bay residents are calling for the state government to provide crucial funding to prevent a hospice facility from closing its doors.

item.title: The disturbing role of ChatGPT in mass shootings
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Oxfam research finds record number of Australian billionaires hold total wealth of $686b
item.description: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

item.title: Iranian state media reports peace talks with US suspended but Trump insists they continue at 'rapid pace'
item.description: Iranian state media is reporting that the country's government has halted negotiations with the United States over an end to the war in the Middle East, in protest over Israel's conduct in Lebanon.

item.title: UK releases more than 1,000 pages of documents relating to Peter Mandelson appointment
item.description: Among the more than 1,000 pages of documents was a note from Peter Mandelson telling the UK foreign secretary at the time that the government would "never regret" giving him the job.

item.title: Carly Sheppard unites with Alisdair Macindoe for The Shepherds at the Australian Dance Biennale
item.description: Carly Sheppard is perhaps best known for experimental performance, but a coincidental meeting with a fellow dancer convinced her to go back to her roots.

item.title: Scripps Spelling Bee quiz: Do you know what these words mean?
item.description: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, never mind how to spell them.

item.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything
item.description: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

item.title: When police charged footballer Tom Starling with assault, one detective broke ranks
item.description: As the footballer, shirt torn, face bloodied and with a concussion, paced his police cell, a veteran detective's phone started to ring.

item.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down
item.description: Fossil fuels, which have powered the global economy for centuries, are under threat. And Australia has become ground zero in this global battle.

item.title: Workmate of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says he's 'given police absolutely everything'
item.description: The last known person to see missing Queensland man Jack McLennan alive has broken his public silence amid intense community speculation over his role in Mr McLennan's disappearance.

item.title: Tax time 2026: ATO warns millions of Aussies chasing tax deductions to stop with 'unusual' claims
item.description: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tips this tax time, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

item.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand US submarines under AUKUS
item.description: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.

item.title: Even Hanson knows polling isn't everything but major parties have their work cut out
item.description: The One Nation leader, whose popularity has soared in the past 12 months, would be the first to say she doesn’t put too much stock in the numbers.

item.title: Year 3 NAPLAN tests stolen from FedEx van in Melbourne
item.description: Dozens of primary school students are victims of an analogue data breach after their NAPLAN tests were stolen from a van in Melbourne's inner-north on April Fool's Day.

item.title: Birds return to Little Dog Island in Bass Strait after feral cats eradicated
item.description: Penguins have been sighted on a remote Bass Strait island for the first time in decades after the successful eradication of feral cats and possums.

item.title: Antibiotic florfenicol detected at 'low level' 14km from fish farm site where it was used
item.description: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

item.title: Voters consider alternatives to major political parties as One Nation popularity surges
item.description: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populist party is leading opinion polls for the first time, and regional Queenslanders say they can see why.

item.title: Emirates proposes increase in helicopter flights at luxury Wolgan Valley resort
item.description: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

item.title: Former wife of tobacco baron alleges horrific life entangled in illegal trade
item.description: After an arranged marriage to tobacco baron Sam Albion, Kawther Salih says the relationship spiralled into an abusive situation that saw her subjugated into working in the underground tobacco industry.

item.title: Ellen Fanning receives a call from "Daniel"
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Daniel' is a fake AI real estate agent who is trying to sell units to strangers
item.description: "Daniel" is an AI real estate agent who calls strangers and secretly records conversations without their consent.

item.title: Tough times for hospitality businesses with rising cost of food and power
item.description: The hospitality sector is grappling with "unprecedented" cost increases, according to a national peak body, with many being forced to close their doors.

item.title: Ipswich deputy mayor Nicole Jonic engaged in misconduct over confidential documents, tribunal rules
item.description: What began as an argument over Paul Pisasale's name on a local bridge evolved into allegations, tribunal hearings and political infighting. A deputy mayor has now been found guilty of misconduct.

item.title: Vacant properties across Sydney used as 'meanwhile use' transitional housing for women in need
item.description: A centuries-old mansion in Sydney became Julie's safe haven, following a series of difficult life circumstances. She is one of many women being assisted through meanwhile use housing.

item.title: Laos cave rescue team member's journey from soldier to comedian to diver
item.description: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

item.title: Council uses AI to inform 10-year plan for 'more depth' at 'fifth of the price'
item.description: A Tasmanian council says using AI for a strategic plan has saved them time and money, and will be used much more in the future, but a resident says the community consultation is 'completely lost with AI'.

item.title: Serena Williams announces return to professional tennis with Queen's Club doubles wildcard
item.description: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

item.title: Katherine residents say potential damages from PFAS firefighting foam case should pay for further 'recompense'
item.description: Residents in Katherine have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".
